source: CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
title: Ensure That Cloud SQL Database Instances Do Not Have Public IPs

input_row.source: CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
input_row.title: Ensure That Cloud SQL Database Instances Do Not Have Public IPs
input_row.description: It is recommended to configure Second Generation Sql instance to use private IPs instead of public IPs.
input_row.rationale: To lower the organization's attack surface, Cloud SQL databases should not have public IPs. Private IPs provide improved network security and lower latency for your application. Impact: Removing the public IP address on SQL instances may break some applications that relied on it for database connectivity.
input_row.audit: From Google Cloud Console 1. Go to the Cloud SQL Instances page in the Google Cloud Console: https://console.cloud.google.com/sql/instances 2. Ensure that every instance has a private IP address and no public IP address configured. From Google Cloud CLI 1. List all Cloud SQL database instances using the following command: gcloud sql instances list 2. For every instance of type instanceType: CLOUD_SQL_INSTANCE with backendType: SECOND_GEN, get detailed configuration. Ignore instances of type READ_REPLICA_INSTANCE because these instances inherit their settings from the primary instance. Also, note that first generation instances cannot be configured to have a private IP address. gcloud sql instances describe <INSTANCE_NAME>  3. Ensure that the setting ipAddresses has an IP address configured of type: PRIVATE and has no IP address of type: PRIMARY. PRIMARY IP addresses are public addresses. An instance can have both a private and public address at the same time. Note also that you cannot use private IP with First Generation instances.
input_row.remediation: From Google Cloud Console 1. Go to the Cloud SQL Instances page in the Google Cloud Console: https://console.cloud.google.com/sql/instances 2. Click the instance name to open its Instance details page. 3. Select the Connections tab. 4. Deselect the Public IP checkbox. 5. Click Save to update the instance. From Google Cloud CLI 1. For every instance remove its public IP and assign a private IP instead: gcloud sql instances patch <INSTANCE_NAME> --network=<VPC_NETWORK_NAME> --no- assign-ip 2. Confirm the changes using the following command:: gcloud sql instances describe <INSTANCE_NAME> Prevention: To prevent new SQL instances from getting configured with public IP addresses, set up a Restrict Public IP access on Cloud SQL instances Organization policy at: https://console.cloud.google.com/iam-admin/orgpolicies/sql-restrictPublicIp. Default Value: By default, Cloud Sql instances have a public IP. References: 1. https://cloud.google.com/sql/docs/mysql/configure-private-ip 2. https://cloud.google.com/sql/docs/mysql/private-ip 3. https://cloud.google.com/resource-manager/docs/organization-policy/org-policy- constraints 4. https://console.cloud.google.com/iam-admin/orgpolicies/sql-restrictPublicIp Additional Information: Replicas inherit their private IP status from their primary instance. You cannot configure a private IP directly on a replica.
input_row.impact: Removing the public IP address on SQL instances may break some applications that relied on it for database connectivity.
input_row.references: 1. https://cloud.google.com/sql/docs/mysql/configure-private-ip 2. https://cloud.google.com/sql/docs/mysql/private-ip 3. https://cloud.google.com/resource-manager/docs/organization-policy/org-policy- constraints 4. https://console.cloud.google.com/iam-admin/orgpolicies/sql-restrictPublicIp Additional Information: Replicas inherit their private IP status from their primary instance. You cannot configure a private IP directly on a replica.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**

The audit requirements indicate that the control check can be fully automated using GCP APIs and the Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) as outlined in the audit and remediation sections. The control involves checking Cloud SQL instances for the presence of public IP addresses, which can be accessed and described via specific gcloud CLI commands. The ability to enumerate instances and verify their IP configuration using automated tools satisfies the criteria for an automated approach. 

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_sql_instance_no_public_ip

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**

- **GCP API Calls/Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) SDK Methods**: The Google Cloud SDK provides commands to interact with Cloud SQL resources.
  
- **GCP CLI Commands**: 
  - Use `gcloud sql instances list` to enumerate all Cloud SQL instances.
  - For each Cloud SQL instance (type `CLOUD_SQL_INSTANCE` and `backendType` `SECOND_GEN`), execute `gcloud sql instances describe <INSTANCE_NAME>` to retrieve instance configuration details.
  
- **Specific Configuration Properties**:
  - Check the `ipAddresses` field within the instance description.
  - Ensure there is an IP address of type `PRIVATE` and verify there is no IP address of type `PRIMARY`.
  - Instances should not have a public IP (`PRIMARY` IP).

- **Pass/Fail Criteria**:
  - **Pass**: A Cloud SQL instance has ***only*** a private IP address configured.
  - **Fail**: A Cloud SQL instance has a public IP address (`PRIMARY` IP address) configured.

These procedures will allow the audit to be conducted programmatically across all Cloud SQL instances to ensure compliance with the control that they do not have public IPs, thus minimizing security risks associated with public exposure.
step2_review:
---

### 1. **APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE

- **Manual vs Automated Classification:**  
  The classification as "Automated" is correct. The presence or absence of public IPs on Cloud SQL instances is fully discoverable via GCP APIs and the Google Cloud SDK (gcloud). No manual review is required if the automation is implemented correctly.
- **GCP API/SDK/CLI Capability:**  
  The assessment correctly identifies that `gcloud sql instances list` and `gcloud sql instances describe <INSTANCE_NAME>` can be used to enumerate and inspect all Cloud SQL instances and their IP configurations.  
  Additionally, the REST API (`projects.instances.list` and `projects.instances.get`) can also be used for automation.
- **Other Tools:**  
  GCP Config (now called "Config Connector") or Security Command Center can also be used, but the gcloud CLI/API approach is sufficient and standard.

---

### 2. **TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 8/10

- **Automation Details:**  
  The overall automation flow is correct: enumerate instances, describe each, and inspect the `ipAddresses` field.
- **gcloud CLI Commands:**  
  - `gcloud sql instances list` — correct for enumeration.
  - `gcloud sql instances describe <INSTANCE_NAME>` — correct for detailed inspection.
- **Field Inspection:**  
  - The `ipAddresses` field contains objects with `type` values: `PRIMARY` (public), `PRIVATE`, and sometimes `OUTGOING`.
  - The assessment says: "verify there is no IP address of type PRIMARY." This is correct for detecting public IPs.
  - However, the statement "Ensure there is an IP address of type PRIVATE" is not strictly required by the CIS control (which only requires *no public IP*, not *must have private IP*). Some instances may be intentionally isolated and have no IP at all (rare, but possible).
- **Pass/Fail Criteria:**  
  - The "Pass" definition ("***only*** a private IP") is stricter than the CIS control, which only requires *no public IP*. The control does not require *at least one private IP*.
- **Program Name:**  
  - `gcp_sql_instance_no_public_ip` is clear, but could be more descriptive (see below).

---

### 3. **IMPROVEMENTS:**

- **Criteria Correction:**  
  - The "Pass" condition should be: "A Cloud SQL instance has **no IP address of type PRIMARY** (public IP) configured."  
    It does **not** require that a private IP is present; just that no public IP is present.
- **Edge Cases:**  
  - Consider instances with *no* IP addresses (rare, but possible). These should also pass, as they are not publicly accessible.
- **Alternative API Methods:**  
  - For large environments, using the REST API (`projects.instances.list`) is more scalable than iterating with CLI.
  - Security Command Center asset inventory can also be used for this check.
- **Program Name Suggestion:**  
  - For clarity and convention, use:  
    `gcp_sql_instance_no_public_ip_assigned`  
    or  
    `gcp_sql_instance_public_ip_disabled`
- **Additional Best Practice:**  
  - Consider flagging instances with both public and private IPs, as this is a common misconfiguration.
- **Documentation:**  
  - Reference the exact field in the API response: `ipAddresses[].type` for clarity.

---

### 4. **CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

- The approach is valid, the technical details are mostly correct, and the audit can be fully automated with GCP CLI/API.

---

### 5. **FINAL RECOMMENDATION:** MODIFY

- **Modify** the pass/fail criteria to match the CIS control:  
  - **Pass:** No `ipAddresses[].type == "PRIMARY"`  
  - **Fail:** Any `ipAddresses[].type == "PRIMARY"`
- **Update the program name** to be more descriptive, e.g., `gcp_sql_instance_public_ip_disabled`.
- **Clarify** that the presence of a private IP is not required by the control, only the absence of a public IP.

---

#### **Summary Table**

| Section               | Verdict/Score | Notes                                                                                   |
|-----------------------|--------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Approach Validation   | AGREE        | Automated is correct; GCP CLI/API fully supports this check.                           |
| Technical Accuracy    | 8/10         | Pass/fail logic is slightly stricter than the CIS control; otherwise technically sound.|
| Improvements          | -            | Loosen pass criteria, clarify edge cases, improve program name.                        |
| Confidence            | HIGH         |                                                                                        |
| Final Recommendation  | MODIFY       | Adjust criteria and naming as above.                                                   |

---
